methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cobod, a Series A-stage housing and construction company founded by Henrik Lund-Nielsen, has raised $46M to date. The comparable universe is limited, consisting of two private peers in the frontier-tech construction sector (Inversion Space and Material Evolution) with no applicable public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

The valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions. Using the $46M total raised as a baseline proxy, this approach yields an implied valuation of $92M. The constrained methodology landscape—driven by limited peer data and the nascent nature of frontier construction technology—limits our ability to triangulate across multiple approaches. Stage-progression mechanics alone provide directional support but cannot be independently validated against revenue multiples, DCF frameworks, or robust peer comparables.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $78M (low) to $106M (high), with $92M as the median. The range width reflects inherent uncertainty rather than methodological diversity. Key uncertainties include: the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this subsector, the availability and relevance of private peer valuations, and the absence of public market reference points. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending deeper diligence on unit economics, technology differentiation, and market adoption metrics specific to frontier construction.
